questions.textHtml: Which service is built on Apache Spark and is compatible with other cloud providers?
questions.rationale:
Databricks is used for processing large amounts of data, which is supported by multiple cloud providers. Data Factory is used to run ETL pipelines. Azure Synapse Analytics is an Azure native service built on Apache Spark. HDInsight is used to process large amounts of data by using Apache Hadoop.

questions.text: Which type of data store uses star schemas, fact tables, and dimension tables?
questions.textHtml: Which type of data store uses star schemas, fact tables, and dimension tables?
questions.rationale:
Data warehouses use fact and dimension tables in a star/snowflake schema. Relational databases do not use fact and dimension tables. Cubes are generated from a data warehouse but are a table themselves. Data lakes store files.

questions.rationaleHtml:
Data warehouses use fact and dimension tables in a star/snowflake schema. Relational databases do not use fact and dimension tables. Cubes are generated from a data warehouse but are a table themselves. Data lakes store files.

questions.name: 4.1_dal_DP900PA
questions.text: Which three actions can you perform directly from an Azure Databricks notebook? Each correct answer presents a complete solution.
questions.textHtml: Which three actions can you perform directly from an Azure Databricks notebook? Each correct answer presents a complete solution.
questions.rationale:
**Objective:**

4.1 Describe common elements of large-scale analytics

**What This Item Tests:**

Describe Microsoft cloud services for large-scale data analytics, including Azure Databricks and Microsoft Fabric.

**Additional Reading:**

[Databricks notebooks - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/notebooks/)  
  
[Understand key concepts | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/explore-azure-databricks/04-key-concepts)  

**Rationale:**

Databricks notebooks are interactive development environments that enable users to execute code and SQL statements directly against the data in a lakehouse. From within a notebook, you can run queries to explore and analyze data, create tables, such as Delta tables, to persist structured data, and create databases in the metastore by running SQL commands. These actions represent core, in-notebook capabilities that support large-scale analytics workloads. In contrast, creating an additional notebook is a workspace management task performed through the Databricks UI, not from within a running notebook. In addition, creating a schema is intentionally excluded at the DP-900 level to avoid unnecessary conceptual overlap with databases, which are the primary organizational construct emphasized for foundational learners.
questions.rationaleHtml:
Objective:

4.1 Describe common elements of large-scale analytics

What This Item Tests:

Describe Microsoft cloud services for large-scale data analytics, including Azure Databricks and Microsoft Fabric.

Additional Reading:

Databricks notebooks - Training | Microsoft Learn

Understand key concepts | Microsoft Learn

Rationale:

Databricks notebooks are interactive development environments that enable users to execute code and SQL statements directly against the data in a lakehouse. From within a notebook, you can run queries to explore and analyze data, create tables, such as Delta tables, to persist structured data, and create databases in the metastore by running SQL commands. These actions represent core, in-notebook capabilities that support large-scale analytics workloads. In contrast, creating an additional notebook is a workspace management task performed through the Databricks UI, not from within a running notebook. In addition, creating a schema is intentionally excluded at the DP-900 level to avoid unnecessary conceptual overlap with databases, which are the primary organizational construct emphasized for foundational learners.

questions.text: What is a characteristic of batch processing?
questions.textHtml: What is a characteristic of batch processing?
questions.rationale:
Batch processing is used to execute complex analysis. Batch processing handles a large amount of data at a time. Batch processing is usually measured in minutes and hours.

questions.name: 4.2_dbj_DP900PA
questions.text:
You need to ingest, transform, and visualize data from a continuously generated data source. The solution must minimize latency and administrative effort.

Which type of data processing should you use?
questions.textHtml:
You need to ingest, transform, and visualize data from a continuously generated data source. The solution must minimize latency and administrative effort.

Which type of data processing should you use?
questions.rationale:
**Objective:**

4.2 Describe consideration for real-time data analytics

**What This Item Tests:**

Describe the difference between batch and streaming data

**Additional Reading:**

[Understand batch and stream processing - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/explore-fundamentals-stream-processing/2-batch-stream)  
  
[Stream processing systems | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/cmu-message-queues-streams/3-stream-processing-systems)  
  
[Batch vs. streaming - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/data-engineering/batch-vs-streaming#batch-vs-streaming)  
  
[Streaming dataflows (preview) - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/transform-model/dataflows/dataflows-streaming)  
  
[Continuous data ingestion - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/data-explorer/ingest-data-overview#continuous-data-ingestion)  

**Rationale:**

Stream processing supports continuously generated data by processing events as they occur, which enables low-latency ingestion, transformation, and visualization while requiring less operational management than interval-based approaches. Batch processing introduces delays because data is collected and processed at scheduled times, and microbatching still processes data in small intervals rather than immediately. Although the term “real-time” is commonly used to describe low-latency scenarios, it is not a formal data processing model; stream processing is the appropriate classification for this type of workload.
questions.rationaleHtml:
Objective:

4.2 Describe consideration for real-time data analytics

What This Item Tests:

Describe the difference between batch and streaming data

Additional Reading:

Understand batch and stream processing - Training | Microsoft Learn

Stream processing systems | Microsoft Learn

Batch vs. streaming - Training | Microsoft Learn

Streaming dataflows (preview) - Training | Microsoft Learn

Continuous data ingestion - Training | Microsoft Learn

Rationale:

Stream processing supports continuously generated data by processing events as they occur, which enables low-latency ingestion, transformation, and visualization while requiring less operational management than interval-based approaches. Batch processing introduces delays because data is collected and processed at scheduled times, and microbatching still processes data in small intervals rather than immediately. Although the term “real-time” is commonly used to describe low-latency scenarios, it is not a formal data processing model; stream processing is the appropriate classification for this type of workload.

questions.name: 4.3_dcc_DP900PA
questions.text: What should you create in a data model to allow users to drill up and drill down in a report?
questions.textHtml: What should you create in a data model to allow users to drill up and drill down in a report?
questions.rationale:
A hierarchy enables drill up and drill down in a dimension. A dimension enables navigation, but hierarchy is used to drill up and down in a dimension. Fact tables have values. Cubes are not created in Microsoft PowerBI.

[Describe core concepts of data modeling - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-fundamentals-data-visualization/3-data-modeling)

questions.rationale:
Line charts are used to compare categorized values, often to show trends over time. Scatter plots are used to compare two numeric measures and identify a relationship or correlation between them. Pie charts are used to show categories as a proportion of a total. Tables are used to display multiple related values.

questions.name: 2.1_baa_DP900PA
questions.text: Which two keys are needed to create a one-to-many relationship between two tables in a relational database? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
questions.textHtml: Which two keys are needed to create a one-to-many relationship between two tables in a relational database? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
questions.rationale:
A primary key and a foreign key are needed for a one-to-many relationship. You need a primary key that must be unique but is not just a unique key. Index keys are used for searches.

[Understand normalization - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-relational-data-offerings/3-normalization?ns-enrollment-type=learningpath&ns-enrollment-id=learn.wwl.azure-data-fundamentals-explore-relational-data)
questions.rationaleHtml:
A primary key and a foreign key are needed for a one-to-many relationship. You need a primary key that must be unique but is not just a unique key. Index keys are used for searches.

questions.name: 2.1_bac_DP900PA
questions.text: What are two advantages of using normalization over not using normalization in a relational database? Each correct answer presents a complete solution.
questions.textHtml: What are two advantages of using normalization over not using normalization in a relational database? Each correct answer presents a complete solution.
questions.rationale:
Normalization uses the least possible amount of storage. Normalization optimizes for updates, inserts, and deletes. Normalization does not optimize for complex reads and does not provide a mechanism for unstructured data.

[Understand normalization - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-relational-data-offerings/3-normalization)
questions.rationaleHtml:
Normalization uses the least possible amount of storage. Normalization optimizes for updates, inserts, and deletes. Normalization does not optimize for complex reads and does not provide a mechanism for unstructured data.

Understand normalization - Training | Microsoft Learn

questions.name: 2.1_bad_DP900PA
questions.text: Which SQL operation is used to combine the content of two tables based on a shared column?
questions.textHtml: Which SQL operation is used to combine the content of two tables based on a shared column?
questions.rationale:
JOIN is used to combine data from two tables based on a shared key. HAVING is used to filter content from a GROUP BY command. UNION displays the content of two sets of columns from two tables but is not based on a shared key. INTERSECT shows only values that exist in both tables.

[Explore SQL - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-relational-data-offerings/4-query-with-sql?ns-enrollment-type=learningpath&ns-enrollment-id=learn.wwl.azure-data-fundamentals-explore-relational-data)

[SELECT (Transact-SQL) - SQL Server | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/sql/t-sql/queries/select-transact-sql?view=sql-server-ver16)
questions.rationaleHtml:
JOIN is used to combine data from two tables based on a shared key. HAVING is used to filter content from a GROUP BY command. UNION displays the content of two sets of columns from two tables but is not based on a shared key. INTERSECT shows only values that exist in both tables.

Explore SQL - Training | Microsoft Learn

SELECT (Transact-SQL) - SQL Server | Microsoft Learn

questions.textHtml: Which SQL clause can be used to copy all the rows from one table to a new table?
questions.rationale:
SELECT - INTO does an insert into a table. SELECT – OVER determines the partitioning and ordering of the rowset before a windowing function is applied. INSERT – VALUES inserts values into a single row. SELECT – HAVING filters data.

questions.rationaleHtml:

questions.text: Which service is managed and serverless, avoids the use of Windows Server licenses, and allows for each workload to have its own instance of the service being used?
questions.textHtml: Which service is managed and serverless, avoids the use of Windows Server licenses, and allows for each workload to have its own instance of the service being used?
questions.rationale:
Azure SQL Database is a serverless platform as a service (PaaS) SQL instance. SQL Managed Instance is a PaaS service, but databases are maintained in the same SQL Managed Instance cluster. SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines running Windows or Linux are not serverless options.

[Identify data services - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-roles-responsibilities-world-of-data/3-data-services)

questions.text: Which SQL engine is optimized for IoT scenarios?
questions.textHtml: Which SQL engine is optimized for IoT scenarios?
questions.rationale:
SQL Edge is optimized for IoT scenarios that must work with streaming time series data. SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machinesis is best used when you want to retain control over the server and database configuration. SQL Managed Instance is ideal for cloud migrations where you need minimal change to existing apps. Azure SQL Database is best used for new cloud solutions.

[Describe Azure SQL services and capabilities - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-provision-deploy-relational-database-offerings-azure/2-azure-sql)

questions.name: 3.1_cad_DP900PA
questions.text: Which type of Azure Storage is used to store large amounts of files to be shared with virtual machines by using SMB?
questions.textHtml: Which type of Azure Storage is used to store large amounts of files to be shared with virtual machines by using SMB?
questions.rationale:
Azure Files is used to share files by using NFS and SMB. Data Lake Storage Gen2 is used for storing huge amounts of data to be processed, not to be shared among virtual machines. Page blobs are used for VHDs. Table storage is used for two dimensional tables.

questions.name: 3.1_cak_DP900PA
questions.text: Which Azure Blob Storage feature prevents blobs from being modified or deleted for a specified retention period?
questions.textHtml: Which Azure Blob Storage feature prevents blobs from being modified or deleted for a specified retention period?
questions.rationale:
**Objective:**

3.1 Describe the capabilities of Azure storage

**What This Item Tests:**

Describe features of Azure Blob storage

**Additional Reading:**

[Security - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/well-architected/service-guides/azure-blob-storage#security)

[Data protection | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/security-recommendations#data-protection)

**Rationale:**

Blob Storage supports immutable blobs, which use time-based retention policies to prevent data from being modified or deleted for a specified period. This is commonly used in compliance and data protection scenarios, where data integrity must be preserved. Legal holds prevent deletion but do not enforce time-based immutability, while soft delete and point-in-time restore enable recovery after deletion or modification rather than preventing those actions. Therefore, immutable blobs provide the strongest protection against modification or deletion.
questions.rationaleHtml:
Objective:

3.1 Describe the capabilities of Azure storage

What This Item Tests:

Describe features of Azure Blob storage

Additional Reading:

Security - Training | Microsoft Learn

Data protection | Microsoft Learn

Rationale:

Blob Storage supports immutable blobs, which use time-based retention policies to prevent data from being modified or deleted for a specified period. This is commonly used in compliance and data protection scenarios, where data integrity must be preserved. Legal holds prevent deletion but do not enforce time-based immutability, while soft delete and point-in-time restore enable recovery after deletion or modification rather than preventing those actions. Therefore, immutable blobs provide the strongest protection against modification or deletion.

questions.name: 1.1_aab_DP900PA
questions.text: Which type of data structure allows you to store data in a two-column format without requiring a complex database management system?
questions.textHtml: Which type of data structure allows you to store data in a two-column format without requiring a complex database management system?
questions.rationale:
A key/value store is used for simple lookups based on a single key to obtain a single value. A relational database is the best option for create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) operations and uses the least amount of storage space, but our solution does not require a database management system (DBMS) on the browser. A document database used unstructured data such as JSON, and optimized for retrieval, not CRUD operations. A graph database is used to store hierarchical data, such as organizational charts that have nodes and edges.

[Explore databases - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-core-data-concepts/4-databases?ns-enrollment-type=learningpath&ns-enrollment-id=learn.wwl.azure-data-fundamentals-explore-core-data-concepts)

A document database used unstructured data such as JSON, and optimized for retrieval, not CRUD operations. A graph database is used to store hierarchical data, such as organizational charts that have nodes and edges.

questions.name: 1.2_abd_DP900PA
questions.text:
You design an application that needs to store data based on the following requirements:

- Store historical data from multiple data sources.
- Load data on a scheduled basis.
- Use a denormalized star or snowflake schema.

Which type of database should you use?
questions.textHtml:
You design an application that needs to store data based on the following requirements:

  • Store historical data from multiple data sources.
  • Load data on a scheduled basis.
  • Use a denormalized star or snowflake schema.

Which type of database should you use?
questions.rationale:
OLAP databases are used for snowflake schemas with historical data. Table storage is not suited for this type of data. Graph databases are used to store hierarchical data, such as organizational charts that have nodes and edges. OLTP databases are used for create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) operations, use the least amount of storage space, and are not for historical data in a snowflake schema.

questions.name: 1.2_abi_DP900PA
questions.text: Which Azure SQL Database feature ensures that users can see only their own rows when multiple customers share the same tables?
questions.textHtml: Which Azure SQL Database feature ensures that users can see only their own rows when multiple customers share the same tables?
questions.rationale:
**Objective:**

1.2 Identify options for data storage

**What This Item Tests:**

Identify Azure datastores for common use cases

**Additional Reading:**

[Row-level security - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/security/row-level-security)

**Rationale:**

Azure SQL Database supports RLS, which restricts access to rows in a table based on a user’s identity or execution context. This enables multiple customers to share the same tables, while ensuring that each customer can see only their own data. Dynamic data masking hides sensitive values but does not prevent access to rows, Always Encrypted protects data at rest and in use but does not filter query results, and TDE protects data at rest only. Therefore, RLS is the correct feature.
questions.rationaleHtml:
Objective:

1.2 Identify options for data storage

What This Item Tests:

Identify Azure datastores for common use cases

Additional Reading:

Row-level security - Training | Microsoft Learn

Rationale:

Azure SQL Database supports RLS, which restricts access to rows in a table based on a user’s identity or execution context. This enables multiple customers to share the same tables, while ensuring that each customer can see only their own data. Dynamic data masking hides sensitive values but does not prevent access to rows, Always Encrypted protects data at rest and in use but does not filter query results, and TDE protects data at rest only. Therefore, RLS is the correct feature.

questions.name: 1.3_acf_DP900PA
questions.text: Which feature of transactional data processing guarantees that concurrent processes cannot see the data in an inconsistent state?
questions.textHtml: Which feature of transactional data processing guarantees that concurrent processes cannot see the data in an inconsistent state?
questions.rationale:
Isolation in transactional data processing ensures that concurrent transactions cannot interfere with one another and must result in a consistent database state.  
Atomicity: A transaction must be treated as a single, indivisible unit, it either completes fully or not at all.   
Consistency: A transaction must bring the database from one valid state to another valid state, maintaining all defined rules, constraints, and data integrity.   
Durability: Once a transaction is committed, its changes are permanent, even if the system crashes immediately afterward.  

[Explore transactional data processing - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-core-data-concepts/5-transactional-data-processing)

[Explore analytical data processing - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-core-data-concepts/6-analytical-processing)
questions.rationaleHtml:
Isolation in transactional data processing ensures that concurrent transactions cannot interfere with one another and must result in a consistent database state.
Atomicity: A transaction must be treated as a single, indivisible unit, it either completes fully or not at all.
Consistency: A transaction must bring the database from one valid state to another valid state, maintaining all defined rules, constraints, and data integrity.
Durability: Once a transaction is committed, its changes are permanent, even if the system crashes immediately afterward.

Explore transactional data processing - Training | Microsoft Learn

Explore analytical data processing - Training | Microsoft Learn
